Recorded vendor KeyHashes for the canonical keyed instances.
Captured 2026-06-22 on host "codepit" (Debian 13, x86_64).

Vendor versions
---------------
  CycloneDDS   : libddsc DDS_VERSION 11.0.1  (idlc + dds_cdrstream API)
  RTI Connext  : 7.7.0  (rtiddsgen 4.7.0, lib arch x64Linux4gcc8.5.0)
  Fast DDS     : 3.6.1.0  (libfastdds.so.3.6.1.0 + libfastcdr.so.2.3.5)
  ZeroDDS      : this repo, crates/cdr key_hash.rs + idl-rust codegen

Instances (identical across all four implementations)
-----------------------------------------------------
  (a) SingleKey { id = 0x11223344 }
  (b) BigKey    { name = "sensor-array-north-wing", region = 7 }
  (c) MixedKeys { a = 0xAA, b = 0x1234, c = 0x55667788, d = 0x5A ('Z') }
  (d) OuterKey  { k = NestedKey { hi = 0x01020304, lo = 0x05060708 } }

How each vendor value was obtained
----------------------------------
  CycloneDDS : dds_stream_write (XCDR2 LE) -> dds_stream_extract_keyBE_from_data
               (BE key, member-id order, same engine CycloneDDS uses on the
               wire), then the <=16-pad / >16-MD5 rule. CycloneDDS does NOT
               emit PID_KEY_HASH inline on the wire by default (verified by
               tcpdump: no 0x0070 in DATA inlineQos), so its key is read from
               the public dds_cdrstream API instead. Source: cyclone_keyhash.c
  RTI Connext: real on-the-wire capture. rti_pubsub publishes one instance of
               each type over UDPv4 loopback; tcpdump captures lo; tshark reads
               the PID_KEY_HASH (0x0070) parameter from the DATA submessage
               inlineQos. RTI emits the keyhash inline by default. This is the
               authoritative wire value. Source: rti_pubsub.cxx, capture rti.pcap
  Fast DDS   : Fast DDS' own FastCDR (Cdr, BIG_ENDIANNESS, XCDRv2, no
               encapsulation) serializes the @key members exactly as the
               fastddsgen-generated compute_key() does; the <=16-pad / >16-MD5
               decision uses Fast DDS' own eprosima::fastdds::MD5 class.
               (No fastddsgen binary on host, so the generated compute_key path
               is reproduced with vendor FastCDR + vendor MD5.) NOTE: the
               alignment-pad byte must be pre-zeroed; FastCDR does not zero it
               (see README finding). Source: fastdds_keyhash.cpp

16-byte KeyHash (hex)  -- all four columns are byte-identical
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
case | ZeroDDS                          | Cyclone | RTI(wire) | FastDDS | branch
-----+----------------------------------+---------+-----------+---------+--------
 (a) | 11223344000000000000000000000000 | MATCH   | MATCH     | MATCH   | zero-pad
 (b) | 492ce15eccc6c49993afe63223435b91 | MATCH   | MATCH     | MATCH   | MD5
 (c) | aa001234556677885a00000000000000 | MATCH   | MATCH     | MATCH   | zero-pad
 (d) | 01020304050607080000000000000000 | MATCH   | MATCH     | MATCH   | zero-pad

Raw BE keyholder bytes that feed case (b)'s MD5 (identical, all impls):
  00 00 00 18 | "sensor-array-north-wing" | 00 (NUL) | 00 00 00 07
  = 0000001873656e736f722d61727261792d6e6f7274682d77696e670000000007
  MD5(above) = 492ce15eccc6c49993afe63223435b91

RTI wire capture excerpt (tshark, PID_KEY_HASH rendered as "guid:")
-------------------------------------------------------------------
  (a)  guid: 11223344:00000000:00000000:00000000
  (b)  guid: 492ce15e:ccc6c499:93afe632:23435b91
  (c)  guid: aa001234:55667788:5a000000:00000000
  (d)  guid: 01020304:05060708:00000000:00000000

Bounded-string divergence (separate type, see README finding F1)
----------------------------------------------------------------
  Type @final struct BS { @key string<8> code; long payload; }, code="AB".
  BE keyholder = 00 00 00 03 41 42 00  (len=3 BE + "AB" + NUL, max 13 <= 16).
    CycloneDDS (own CDR engine): 00000003414200000000000000000000  (zero-pad)
    ZeroDDS                    : afe1514800e44535d0f8280b7d11b82f  (MD5)  <-- DIFF
  ZeroDDS treats every string key as unbounded -> MD5; the spec + Cyclone
  zero-pad when the bounded max <= 16. These never correlate.
